title: RV8GR store path
purpose: Prove the T2 store control path from AC to IBUS to DBUS to RAM without ROM/RAM/U7 contention.

chips.role: DBUS device disabled by WR_DIR during store
DBUS write target when A15=1 and /WE LOW

behavior.store_enable: /AC_BUF=NAND(T2,STR). It goes LOW only during T2 when STR=1.
behavior.u14: When /AC_BUF=0, U14 drives AC onto IBUS.
behavior.u7: During store, U7 is enabled and WR_DIR=1, so it drives IBUS onto DBUS.
behavior.rom_safety: WR_DIR=1 drives ROM /OE HIGH, disabling ROM output during CPU store.
behavior.ram_safety: RAM /WE follows /AC_BUF; /WE LOW writes and disables RAM output drivers.
behavior.bus_policy: Store phases allow exactly one active IBUS driver, U14, and exactly one active DBUS driver, U7; ROM and RAM outputs must be high-Z before U7 writes DBUS.
behavior.rom_page_store: If A15=0, CPU store targets ROM space. ROM output is disabled; normal EEPROM/flash unlock protection means no ordinary RAM write occurs.

timing.control_window: Store controls are valid only inside T2 with STR=1.
timing.deadband_boundary: The store path requires measured or source-backed output-float deadband, U7 enable delay, and DBUS R/C settling before any physical-speed claim.
unsafe_states: ROM output enabled while U7 writes DBUS
RAM output enabled while U7 writes DBUS

guarantees: T2 with STR=0 never asserts store controls
T2 with STR=1 enables U14, U7 write direction, ROM output disable, and RAM /WE LOW
RAM store writes only when A15=1
ROM-page store does not create a ROM-vs-U7 bus fight

audience: students ages 10-15, still useful for older learners
